Index.title: ATUM — The Root of All Words
Index.subtitle: Every Word Has a Root
Index.description: Discover the electromagnetic root systems that structure all human languages. Learn to decode the patterns of speech and sound.
Index.heroExplanation: Language is not arbitrary. Just as Arabic utilizes root letters to derive thousands of words through defined pattern weights (أوزان), all world languages branch from three fundamental electromagnetic roots: ATUM (unity and inertia), BULL (radiation and expansion), and TOR (rotation and cycle).
Index.atumDesc: Represents inertia, stability, containment, and return. It is the static, inward-looking force.
Index.bullDesc: Represents radiation, expansion, movement, and flowing energy. It is the active, outward-moving force.
Index.torDesc: Represents rotation, cycles, towers, boundaries, and geometric structure.
Index.searchPlaceholder: Search any word to find its root...

ATUM.physics: ATUM represents inertia — the tendency of matter to remain at rest or in uniform motion. Just as the atom is the indivisible unit of matter, أتم means "to complete, make whole." ATUM-root words describe things that are fundamental, unified, and self-contained.

BULL.physics: BULL represents radiation — energy expanding outward from a center. This is the principle of fire, light, and all wave propagation. بول means "to radiate, emit." BULL-root words describe things that expand, radiate, or burst forth.

TOR.physics: TOR represents toroidal/spiral dynamics — the most common energy pattern in the universe. From galaxies to hurricanes to DNA, energy follows toroidal paths. طور means "to turn, cycle." TOR-root words describe things that turn, cycle, or form structures.
